title: Add vectorization pass for Moore dialect
body:
This patch introduces a new transformation pass for the Moore dialect that performs simple vectorization on scalar assignments.

The pass detects sequences of bit-level assignments from input to output signals and replaces them with a single vector assignment, simplifying the IR and generating cleaner Verilog.

**Example**

Given the Verilog design:
```Systemverilog
module simple_vectorization(output wire [3:0] out, input wire [3:0] in);
  assign out[3] = in[3];
  assign out[2] = in[2];
  assign out[1] = in[1];
  assign out[0] = in[0];
endmodule
```


The corresponding Moore IR before vectorization looks like:
```MLIR
module {
  moore.module @simple_vectorization(out out : !moore.l4, in %in : !moore.l4) {
    %out = moore.net wire : <l4>
    %0 = moore.extract_ref %out from 3 : <l4> -> <l1>
    %1 = moore.extract %in from 3 : l4 -> l1
    moore.assign %0, %1 : l1
    %2 = moore.extract_ref %out from 2 : <l4> -> <l1>
    %3 = moore.extract %in from 2 : l4 -> l1
    moore.assign %2, %3 : l1
    %4 = moore.extract_ref %out from 1 : <l4> -> <l1>
    %5 = moore.extract %in from 1 : l4 -> l1
    moore.assign %4, %5 : l1
    %6 = moore.extract_ref %out from 0 : <l4> -> <l1>
    %7 = moore.extract %in from 0 : l4 -> l1
    moore.assign %6, %7 : l1
    %8 = moore.read %out : <l4>
    moore.output %8 : !moore.l4
  }
}
```
After running the VectorizationPass, the IR is simplified to:
```MLIR
module {
  moore.module @simple_vectorization(out out : !moore.l4, in %in : !moore.l4) {
    %out = moore.net wire : <l4>
    moore.assign %out, %in : l4
    %0 = moore.read %out : <l4>
    moore.output %0 : !moore.l4
  }
}
```

Which generates the optimized Verilog:
```Systemverilog
module simple_vectorization(output wire [3:0] out, input wire [3:0] in);
  assign out = in;
endmodule
```

This pass currently handles straightforward, contiguous, and ordered cases only.
It matches extract and connect operations of the form out[i] = in[i] for a range of i.

This is the first vectorization case handled by the pass, future work will extend support to more complex and interleaved patterns.
